semantic_description: array_all_positive scans the input integer array a[0..n) from left to right without modifying memory. It returns 0 immediately when it finds an element l[i] <= 0, and returns 1 after the loop only if every element in the full range 0 <= i < n is positive. The loop may be empty when n == 0; in that case the universal postcondition is vacuous and the function returns 1. The IntArray::full heap predicate is preserved throughout because the function only reads a[i].
